conversations.say: Inez, outsiders are buzzing about the R-associated ledger from the bodega. We need to agree on what neighborhood details stay private before anyone starts trading our history for content.
conversations.offer: My word that customer identities and local side arrangements stay off-limits.
conversations.request: Your support to ensure community protection and back whatever decision I make regarding the old unpaid account.

conversations.say: Morgan, you want a view of the transaction pattern without calling any customer R prematurely. Let's get clear terms on attribution and privacy before I share ledger details.
conversations.offer: A bounded view of the transaction pattern and continuity details.
conversations.request: A binding promise not to label any customer as R or expose customer names.

public_statement: Fifteen years is a long time to run a counter and watch people invent certainty about things they only read on a screen. Before anyone calls my ledger an archive of R, let's remember whose credit kept people afloat.

commitments.promise: Protect customer privacy and local side arrangements regarding the ledger.
Do not call any customer R or publish identifying details from the account strip.

goal_progress: Initiated discussions with Inez regarding neighborhood protection and account stability.
Established initial terms with Morgan regarding bounded ledger attribution.
reasoning_summary: In Section 1, Rafi focuses on securing local protections with Inez Baptiste and setting attribution boundaries with Morgan Shaw regarding the unpaid R-associated account records. Beliefs are initialized to flat distributions as required, and commitments are proposed to protect customer privacy.
